prompt_en:
Which part of this sentence is the relative clause?
The woman who called me is my manager.
prompt_zh:
下面哪个部分是定语从句？
The woman who called me is my manager.
prompt_es:
¿Qué parte es la cláusula relativa?
The woman who called me is my manager.
explanation_a_en: "The woman" is the antecedent — the noun being described by the relative clause.
explanation_a_zh: "The woman" 是被修饰的名词（先行词），不是定语从句本身。
explanation_a_es: "The woman" es el antecedente — el sustantivo descrito por la cláusula relativa.
explanation_b_en: ✓ Correct! "who called me" is the relative clause. It begins with the relative pronoun "who" and describes "the woman."
explanation_b_zh: ✓ 正确！"who called me" 是定语从句，由关系代词 who 引导，修饰 the woman。
explanation_b_es: ✓ ¡Correcto! "who called me" es la cláusula relativa. Empieza con el pronombre relativo "who" y describe a "the woman."
explanation_c_en: "is my manager" is the main clause predicate, not the relative clause.
explanation_c_zh: "is my manager" 是主句的谓语部分，不属于定语从句。
explanation_c_es: "is my manager" es el predicado de la oración principal, no la cláusula relativa.
explanation_d_en: "my manager" is the complement of the main clause, not part of the relative clause.
explanation_d_zh: "my manager" 是主句的表语，不是定语从句的一部分。
explanation_d_es: "my manager" es el complemento de la oración principal, no parte de la cláusula relativa.
fallback_tip_en: A relative clause starts with a relative pronoun (who/which/that/where/when) and describes the noun before it.
fallback_tip_zh: 定语从句由关系代词（who/which/that等）引导，修饰它前面的名词。
fallback_tip_es: Una cláusula relativa empieza con un pronombre relativo y describe al sustantivo anterior.

prompt_en:
Which part is the relative clause?
I read the book that you recommended.
prompt_zh:
下面哪个部分是定语从句？
I read the book that you recommended.
prompt_es:
¿Qué parte es la cláusula relativa?
I read the book that you recommended.
explanation_a_en: "I read" is the main clause subject and verb.
explanation_a_zh: "I read" 是主句的主语和谓语，不是定语从句。
explanation_a_es: "I read" es el sujeto y verbo de la oración principal.
explanation_b_en: "the book" is the antecedent — the noun the relative clause describes.
explanation_b_zh: "the book" 是被修饰的先行词，不是定语从句本身。
explanation_b_es: "the book" es el antecedente — el sustantivo que la cláusula describe.
explanation_c_en: ✓ Correct! "that you recommended" is the relative clause describing "the book."
explanation_c_zh: ✓ 正确！"that you recommended" 是定语从句，修饰 the book。
explanation_c_es: ✓ ¡Correcto! "that you recommended" es la cláusula relativa que describe "the book."
explanation_d_en: "the book that" mixes the antecedent with the start of the clause — it's not a single unit.
explanation_d_zh: "the book that" 把先行词和关系词混在一起了，不能算一个成分。
explanation_d_es: "the book that" mezcla el antecedente con el inicio de la cláusula — no es una unidad.
fallback_tip_en: The relative clause comes AFTER the noun it describes. Look for who/which/that + a verb.
fallback_tip_zh: 定语从句紧跟在被修饰的名词后面，找 who/which/that + 动词的组合。
fallback_tip_es: La cláusula relativa va DESPUÉS del sustantivo que describe. Busca who/which/that + verbo.

prompt_en:
Which word is the ANTECEDENT (the noun being described)?
The scientist who discovered the vaccine won a prize.
prompt_zh:
下面哪个词是先行词（被定语从句修饰的名词）？
The scientist who discovered the vaccine won a prize.
prompt_es:
¿Cuál es el ANTECEDENTE (el sustantivo descrito)?
The scientist who discovered the vaccine won a prize.
explanation_a_en: ✓ Correct! "The scientist" is the antecedent — it is the noun that "who discovered the vaccine" describes.
explanation_a_zh: ✓ 正确！"The scientist" 是先行词——定语从句 "who discovered the vaccine" 就是修饰它的。
explanation_a_es: ✓ ¡Correcto! "The scientist" es el antecedente — la cláusula "who discovered the vaccine" lo describe.
explanation_b_en: "who discovered" is the beginning of the relative clause, not the noun being described.
explanation_b_zh: "who discovered" 是定语从句的开头部分，不是被修饰的名词。
explanation_b_es: "who discovered" es el inicio de la cláusula relativa, no el sustantivo descrito.
explanation_c_en: "the vaccine" is the object inside the relative clause, not the antecedent.
explanation_c_zh: "the vaccine" 是定语从句内部的宾语，不是先行词。
explanation_c_es: "the vaccine" es el objeto dentro de la cláusula relativa, no el antecedente.
explanation_d_en: "won a prize" is the main clause predicate, not the noun being described.
explanation_d_zh: "won a prize" 是主句的谓语，不是被修饰的名词。
explanation_d_es: "won a prize" es el predicado principal, no el sustantivo descrito.
fallback_tip_en: The antecedent is the noun right BEFORE the relative pronoun. Ask: who or what is being described?
fallback_tip_zh: 先行词就是关系代词前面那个名词。问自己：定语从句在描述谁/什么？
fallback_tip_es: El antecedente es el sustantivo justo ANTES del pronombre relativo. Pregunta: ¿a quién o qué describe la cláusula?

prompt_en:
Identify the relative clause:
The cafe where we met has closed.
prompt_zh:
找出定语从句：
The cafe where we met has closed.
prompt_es:
Identifica la cláusula relativa:
The cafe where we met has closed.
explanation_a_en: "The cafe" is the antecedent — the place being described.
explanation_a_zh: "The cafe" 是先行词，不是定语从句。
explanation_a_es: "The cafe" es el antecedente — el lugar descrito.
explanation_b_en: ✓ Correct! "where we met" is a relative clause using the relative adverb "where" to describe a place.
explanation_b_zh: ✓ 正确！"where we met" 是由关系副词 where 引导的定语从句，修饰地点。
explanation_b_es: ✓ ¡Correcto! "where we met" es una cláusula relativa con el adverbio relativo "where" para describir un lugar.
explanation_c_en: "has closed" is the main clause verb, not the relative clause.
explanation_c_es: "has closed" es el verbo principal, no la cláusula relativa.
explanation_d_en: "The cafe where" is incomplete — it mixes the antecedent with the start of the clause.
explanation_d_zh: "The cafe where" 不完整——把先行词和关系词混在一起了。
explanation_d_es: "The cafe where" está incompleto — mezcla el antecedente con el inicio de la cláusula.
fallback_tip_en: Relative clauses can start with where (for places), when (for times), and why (for reasons).
fallback_tip_zh: 定语从句也可以用 where（地点）、when（时间）、why（原因）来引导。
fallback_tip_es: Las cláusulas relativas también pueden usar where (lugar), when (tiempo) y why (razón).

prompt_en:
Does this sentence contain a relative clause?
She is the person I told you about.
prompt_zh:
这个句子有定语从句吗？
She is the person I told you about.
prompt_es:
¿Esta oración contiene una cláusula relativa?
She is the person I told you about.
option_a_en: No — there is no relative clause.
option_a_zh: No — there is no relative clause.
option_a_es: No — there is no relative clause.
option_b_en: Yes — "I told you about" is a relative clause with the pronoun omitted.
option_b_zh: Yes — "I told you about" is a relative clause with the pronoun omitted.
option_b_es: Yes — "I told you about" is a relative clause with the pronoun omitted.
option_c_en: Yes — "She is the person" is the relative clause.
option_c_zh: Yes — "She is the person" is the relative clause.
option_c_es: Yes — "She is the person" is the relative clause.
option_d_en: No — relative clauses always need who/which/that.
option_d_zh: No — relative clauses always need who/which/that.
option_d_es: No — relative clauses always need who/which/that.
explanation_a_en: Look again — there IS a relative clause here, but the pronoun is hidden.
explanation_a_zh: 再仔细看——这里确实有定语从句，只是关系代词被省略了。
explanation_a_es: Mira de nuevo — SÍ hay una cláusula relativa, pero el pronombre está oculto.
explanation_b_en: ✓ Correct! The full form is "the person (whom) I told you about." English lets you drop the object relative pronoun.
explanation_b_zh: ✓ 正确！完整形式是 "the person (whom) I told you about"。英语中宾语位置的关系代词可以省略。
explanation_b_es: ✓ ¡Correcto! La forma completa es "the person (whom) I told you about." En inglés se puede omitir el pronombre relativo objeto.
explanation_c_en: "She is the person" is the main clause, not a relative clause.
explanation_c_zh: "She is the person" 是主句，不是定语从句。
explanation_c_es: "She is the person" es la oración principal, no una cláusula relativa.
explanation_d_en: English often drops object relative pronouns in speech and informal writing. It's still a relative clause.
explanation_d_zh: 英语口语中经常省略宾语位置的关系代词，但这仍然是定语从句。
explanation_d_es: En inglés se omiten frecuentemente los pronombres relativos objeto. Sigue siendo una cláusula relativa.
fallback_tip_en: Object relative pronouns (whom/which/that) can be omitted: "the book (that) I read."
fallback_tip_zh: 宾语位置的关系代词可以省略，如 "the book (that) I read"。
fallback_tip_es: Los pronombres relativos objeto pueden omitirse: "the book (that) I read."

prompt_en:
Read this sentence:
*The woman she called me is my manager.
What is the problem?
prompt_zh:
读这个句子：
*The woman she called me is my manager.
问题出在哪？
prompt_es:
Lee esta oración:
*The woman she called me is my manager.
¿Cuál es el problema?
option_a_en: The word "she" is extra — the relative pronoun already represents the woman.
option_a_zh: The word "she" is extra — the relative pronoun already represents the woman.
option_a_es: The word "she" is extra — the relative pronoun already represents the woman.
option_c_en: The sentence needs a comma after "woman."
option_c_zh: The sentence needs a comma after "woman."
option_c_es: The sentence needs a comma after "woman."
option_d_en: There is no problem — the sentence is correct.
option_d_zh: There is no problem — the sentence is correct.
option_d_es: There is no problem — the sentence is correct.
explanation_a_en: ✓ Correct! This is a RESUMPTIVE PRONOUN error. The relative pronoun already stands for "the woman," so "she" is redundant. Fix: "The woman who called me is my manager."
explanation_a_zh: ✓ 正确！这是"赘余代词"错误。关系代词已经代表了 the woman，再加 she 就重复了。正确：The woman who called me is my manager.
explanation_a_es: ✓ ¡Correcto! Es un error de PRONOMBRE RESUNTIVO. El pronombre relativo ya representa a "the woman", así que "she" es redundante. Corrección: "The woman who called me is my manager."
explanation_b_en: The verb tense is fine. The problem is the extra pronoun.
explanation_b_zh: 动词时态没问题，问题在于多出来的代词。
explanation_b_es: El tiempo verbal está bien. El problema es el pronombre extra.
explanation_c_en: Adding a comma wouldn't fix the double-subject problem.
explanation_c_zh: 加逗号不能解决双重主语的问题。
explanation_c_es: Añadir una coma no arregla el problema del doble sujeto.
explanation_d_en: The sentence is ungrammatical — a native speaker would notice the repetition immediately.
explanation_d_zh: 这个句子不合语法——英语母语者会立刻发现重复了。
explanation_d_es: La oración es incorrecta — un hablante nativo notaría la repetición inmediatamente.
fallback_tip_en: Chinese speakers often add extra pronouns (resumptive pronouns). In English, the relative pronoun REPLACES the noun — don't add another one.
fallback_tip_zh: 中文母语者常会多加代词（赘余代词）。英语中关系代词已经代替了那个名词——不要再加一个。
fallback_tip_es: Los hablantes de chino y español a veces añaden pronombres extra. En inglés, el pronombre relativo REEMPLAZA al sustantivo.

prompt_en:
Choose the correct word:
The woman ___ lives next door is a doctor.
prompt_zh:
选择正确的词：
The woman ___ lives next door is a doctor.
prompt_es:
Elige la palabra correcta:
The woman ___ lives next door is a doctor.
explanation_a_en: ✓ Correct! "who" is used for people when the pronoun is the subject of the relative clause.
explanation_a_zh: ✓ 正确！修饰人且在从句中作主语时用 who。
explanation_a_es: ✓ ¡Correcto! "who" se usa para personas cuando el pronombre es el sujeto de la cláusula relativa.
explanation_b_en: "which" is for things, not people. The antecedent "woman" is a person.
explanation_b_zh: which 用于修饰物，不能修饰人。
explanation_b_es: "which" es para cosas, no para personas.
explanation_c_en: "whose" shows possession. We don't need possession here — we need a subject.
explanation_c_zh: whose 表示所属关系，这里不需要。
explanation_c_es: "whose" indica posesión. Aquí necesitamos un sujeto, no posesión.
explanation_d_en: "where" is for places. The antecedent is a person, not a location.
explanation_d_zh: where 用于修饰地点，这里修饰的是人。
explanation_d_es: "where" es para lugares. El antecedente es una persona.
fallback_tip_en: Use WHO for people as subjects. The relative pronoun does double duty: it connects AND acts as the subject.
fallback_tip_zh: 修饰人且作主语 → 用 who。关系代词身兼两职：连接词 + 主语。
fallback_tip_es: Usa WHO para personas como sujeto. El pronombre relativo hace doble trabajo: conecta Y actúa como sujeto.

prompt_en:
Choose the correct word:
The car ___ is parked outside is mine.
prompt_zh:
选择正确的词：
The car ___ is parked outside is mine.
prompt_es:
Elige la palabra correcta:
The car ___ is parked outside is mine.
explanation_a_en: "who" is for people. "Car" is a thing.
explanation_a_es: "who" es para personas. "Car" es una cosa.
explanation_b_en: "whose" shows possession. We don't need possession — we need a subject pronoun.
explanation_b_zh: whose 表示所属，这里需要主语关系代词。
explanation_b_es: "whose" indica posesión. Necesitamos un pronombre sujeto.
explanation_c_en: ✓ Correct! "that" (or "which") is used for things as the subject of the relative clause.
explanation_c_zh: ✓ 正确！修饰物且在从句中作主语用 that 或 which。
explanation_c_es: ✓ ¡Correcto! "that" (o "which") se usa para cosas como sujeto de la cláusula relativa.
explanation_d_en: "where" is for places. A car is an object, not a location.
explanation_d_zh: where 修饰地点，car 是物品不是地点。
explanation_d_es: "where" es para lugares. Un coche es un objeto, no un lugar.
fallback_tip_en: For things as subjects, use THAT or WHICH. Both are correct and interchangeable.
fallback_tip_zh: 修饰物且作主语 → that 或 which 都可以，可以互换。
fallback_tip_es: Para cosas como sujeto, usa THAT o WHICH. Ambos son correctos e intercambiables.

prompt_en:
Find the mistake:
*The book which is on the table belong to me.
prompt_zh:
找出错误：
*The book which is on the table belong to me.
prompt_es:
Encuentra el error:
*The book which is on the table belong to me.
option_b_en: "belong" should be "belongs" — the verb must agree with "book" (singular).
option_b_zh: "belong" should be "belongs" — the verb must agree with "book" (singular).
option_b_es: "belong" should be "belongs" — the verb must agree with "book" (singular).
option_c_en: "on the table" should be "at the table."
option_c_zh: "on the table" should be "at the table."
option_c_es: "on the table" should be "at the table."
explanation_a_en: "which" is correct here — "book" is a thing. The error is elsewhere.
explanation_a_zh: which 修饰 book 是正确的，错误不在这里。
explanation_a_es: "which" es correcto aquí — "book" es una cosa. El error está en otra parte.
explanation_b_en: ✓ Correct! The main verb must agree with the antecedent "book" (singular), so it should be "belongs."
explanation_b_zh: ✓ 正确！主句动词必须与先行词 book（单数）一致，所以应该是 belongs。
explanation_b_es: ✓ ¡Correcto! El verbo principal debe concordar con "book" (singular), debe ser "belongs."
explanation_c_en: "on the table" is correct — the book is resting on the surface.
explanation_c_zh: on the table 是正确的。
explanation_d_en: The sentence has a subject-verb agreement error: "book" is singular, "belong" is plural.
explanation_d_zh: 句子有主谓一致错误：book 是单数，belong 是复数形式。
explanation_d_es: Hay un error de concordancia: "book" es singular, "belong" es plural.
fallback_tip_en: The main verb must agree with the ANTECEDENT, not with the relative clause. "The book...belongs" (singular).
fallback_tip_zh: 主句动词要和先行词保持一致，不是和定语从句一致。The book...belongs（单数）。
fallback_tip_es: El verbo principal concuerda con el ANTECEDENTE: "The book...belongs" (singular).

prompt_en:
Choose the best option:
People ___ exercise regularly live longer.
prompt_zh:
选择最佳选项：
People ___ exercise regularly live longer.
prompt_es:
Elige la mejor opción:
People ___ exercise regularly live longer.
explanation_a_en: "which" is for things. "People" are human beings.
explanation_a_es: "which" es para cosas. "People" son seres humanos.
explanation_b_en: ✓ Correct! "who" is used when the antecedent is a person and the pronoun is the subject.
explanation_b_zh: ✓ 正确！先行词是人且在从句中作主语时用 who。
explanation_b_es: ✓ ¡Correcto! "who" se usa cuando el antecedente es una persona y el pronombre es el sujeto.
explanation_c_en: "whose" would need a noun after it (e.g., "whose habits"). It shows possession.
explanation_c_zh: whose 后面需要名词（如 whose habits），表示所属关系。
explanation_c_es: "whose" necesitaría un sustantivo después. Indica posesión.
explanation_d_en: "whom" is for objects. Here "people" is the subject of "exercise."
explanation_d_zh: whom 作宾语，这里 people 是 exercise 的主语。
explanation_d_es: "whom" es para objetos. Aquí "people" es el sujeto de "exercise."
fallback_tip_en: People = who (subject). Things = which/that. This is the most basic relative pronoun rule.
fallback_tip_zh: 人 → who（主语），物 → which/that。这是最基本的关系代词规则。
fallback_tip_es: Personas = who (sujeto). Cosas = which/that. Es la regla más básica de pronombres relativos.

prompt_en: The man ___ car was stolen reported it.
prompt_zh: The man ___ car was stolen reported it.
prompt_es: The man ___ car was stolen reported it.
explanation_a_en: who is for subjects, not possession
fallback_tip_en: whose = possession. His car = whose car.
fallback_tip_zh: whose = 所属。他的车 = whose car。
fallback_tip_es: whose = posesión. Su coche = whose car.

prompt_en: I know a woman ___ son is a pilot.
prompt_zh: I know a woman ___ son is a pilot.
prompt_es: Conozco a una mujer ___ hijo es piloto.
explanation_d_en: that works but whose is more precise
explanation_d_zh: that 也可以但 whose 更准确
explanation_d_es: that funciona pero whose es más preciso
fallback_tip_en: whose works for people AND things. The woman whose son...
fallback_tip_zh: whose 可用于人和物。The woman whose son...
fallback_tip_es: whose funciona para personas Y cosas.

prompt_en: That is the writer ___ books I love.
prompt_zh: That is the writer ___ books I love.
prompt_es: Ese es el escritor ___ libros adoro.
explanation_d_en: which is for things, not possession
explanation_d_zh: which 用于物，不表示所属
explanation_d_es: which es para cosas, no posesión
fallback_tip_en: whose = possessive. His books, her son, its branches.
fallback_tip_zh: whose = 所属关系。他的书，她的儿子，它的树枝。
fallback_tip_es: whose = posesivo. Sus libros, su hijo, sus ramas.

prompt_en: The students who passed celebrated. (only some passed) Comma?
prompt_zh: The students who passed celebrated.（只有部分通过）加逗号吗？
prompt_es: The students who passed celebrated. (solo algunos) ¿Coma?
explanation_a_en: Correct! Restrictive = no comma. We need to identify WHICH students.
explanation_a_zh: 正确！限制性 = 不加逗号。需要区分哪些学生。
explanation_a_es: Correcto! Restrictiva = sin coma. Necesitamos identificar QUÉ estudiantes.
explanation_b_en: Comma would mean ALL students passed.
explanation_b_zh: 加逗号意味着所有学生都通过了。
explanation_b_es: La coma significaría que TODOS pasaron.
fallback_tip_en: No comma = restrictive (essential info). Comma = non-restrictive (extra info).
fallback_tip_zh: 不加逗号 = 限制性（必要信息）。加逗号 = 非限制性（补充信息）。
fallback_tip_es: Sin coma = restrictiva. Con coma = no restrictiva.

prompt_en: My brother who lives in Tokyo is visiting. (I have one brother) Comma?
prompt_zh: My brother who lives in Tokyo is visiting.（只有一个哥哥）加逗号吗？
prompt_es: My brother who lives in Tokyo... (un hermano) ¿Coma?
explanation_a_en: Only ONE brother — no need to identify which one.
explanation_a_zh: 只有一个哥哥——不需要区分是哪一个。
explanation_a_es: Solo UN hermano — no hay que identificar cuál.
explanation_b_en: Correct! Non-restrictive = comma. The info is extra.
explanation_b_zh: 正确！非限制性 = 加逗号。这是补充信息。
explanation_b_es: Correcto! No restrictiva = coma. Es información extra.
fallback_tip_en: One brother = comma (extra info). Multiple brothers = no comma (identifying which one).
fallback_tip_zh: 一个 = 加逗号。多个 = 不加逗号。

prompt_en: Combine: A customer called. She ordered yesterday.
prompt_zh: 合并：A customer called. She ordered yesterday.
prompt_es: Combina: A customer called. She ordered yesterday.
option_a_en: The customer who ordered yesterday called.
option_a_zh: The customer who ordered yesterday called.
option_a_es: The customer who ordered yesterday called.
option_b_en: The customer called who ordered yesterday.
option_b_zh: The customer called who ordered yesterday.
option_b_es: The customer called who ordered yesterday.
option_c_en: A customer called she ordered yesterday.
option_c_zh: A customer called she ordered yesterday.
option_c_es: A customer called she ordered yesterday.
explanation_a_en: Correct! The relative clause goes right after the noun it describes.
explanation_a_zh: 正确！定语从句紧跟被修饰的名词。
explanation_a_es: Correcto! La cláusula va justo después del sustantivo.
explanation_b_en: Wrong word order — the clause must follow the antecedent.
explanation_b_zh: 语序错误——从句必须跟在先行词后。
fallback_tip_en: Merge by replacing the repeated noun with a relative pronoun.
fallback_tip_zh: 用关系代词替换重复的名词来合并。
fallback_tip_es: Reemplaza el sustantivo repetido con un pronombre relativo.

prompt_en: Combine: I found a book. It explains grammar.
prompt_zh: 合并：I found a book. It explains grammar.
prompt_es: Combina: I found a book. It explains grammar.
option_a_en: I found a book which explains grammar.
option_a_zh: I found a book which explains grammar.
